item.title: The latest in my Hairstyle Recreation series, hairstyles from video game characters!
item.dc:creator: GingerSnark

item.title: Wow…
item.description: Thought I’d take a break from all the terrible things happening in Ukraine and focus on terrible things happening here for a moment. Even in the best of systems I’m against the death penalty. But our system is so broken that’s even more reason why we shouldn’t have it. It’s crazy how difficult it is to appeal a case once it’s been decided. Even though that appeal process is supposed to be there to catch cases like this. It just isn’t working right now. Likely due to the Antiterrorism and Effective Death Penalty Act (AEDC) which just seems to show that everytime we go after something in vengeance (this was a response to the Oklahoma Bombing) we just make things worse. This case is particularly crazy. Just the fact the D.A. who prosecuted her is current in prison is very telling. I’m not terribly sad I left Texas. Although I really do hope the good people there can do something to change it. Anyways I don’t really have a following or a vibrant social media presence but I thought I’d at least try and get this petition more views.#SaveMelissaLucio Edit: An article that I thought was interesting. Mainly talks of the documentary they did. Is a little dated at this point. https://www.texasmonthly.com/arts-entertainment/did-melissa-lucio-the-first-hispanic-woman-on-death-row-in-texas-kill-her-daughter-an-uneven-new-documentary-raises-more-questions-than-answers/amp/ https://innocenceproject.org/petitions/stop-execution-of-innocent-melissa-lucio-texas/
item.dc:creator: TheRedCat13

item.dc:creator: GlobalImageServiceCat

item.title: Astronomers of Imgur, I may have made a scientific discovery! I have found a predictive method to measure gravitational reflections which could help us understand gravitational lensing. Can you help this citizen scientist get attention to these images?
item.description:
This is the Coma Galaxy Cluster. I have outlined obvious gravitational lensing with colors matching to a corresponding repeated pattern. Gravitational lensing is the mysterious splitting and warping of light and imagery throughout spacetime, theorized to be caused by dark matter. This is known to cause several images of the same spacetime, however the deeper mechanics are still not clear as our understanding of gravitational physics is still incomplete.
Here is the unedited original for contrast. The Coma Cluster is home to plenty of repeat images caused by strong gravitational lensing. This is scientifically verified. Reflections created by gravitational lensing are typically visible in a line across the lens center of mass. This is the fundamental rule my measurement system is based on. Now I will introduce you to my method of measurement: the mirrored composite.
If one copies an image of outer spacetime and places it upon itself at half transparency, then flips the pixels in the layer in one axis, such as this image where the layer is flipped top-to-bottom, it should hypothetically naturally overlay any related reflections of one side of the gravitational lens upon its twin on the other side. Here's a demonstration with regular reflections on glass:
Both glasses have reflections on the surface, however the source of that light is the same, just warped by the underlying structure of the glass. Here's what it looks like with the composite process:
The process has effectively clumped the similar reflections together, and in so doing emphasized their relationship to each other. This is exactly what my composites of space are attempting to do; emphasize potential duplicate imagery that's not already identified. What evidence do I have that this actually works? Though the composites create artificial symmetry in one axis, the other axis is free from artificial duplication.. yet you still find alternate angles of the same structure, including identical star and galaxy patterns.
This composite is mirrored left-to-right, yet if you trace your finger down the mirrored fold in the center, you will notice going up and down that the structure is warped but identical. This works so consistently well that despite hundreds of composites, I have no examples yet where it fails to match along the fold, including at the scale of the Milky Way. Look at the coma cluster composite again, and this time look for the symmetry along the folded axis.
This image is overlaid top-over-bottom, so the white line indicates the axis where artificial duplicates live. Right now the white axis is unimportant. What you want to focus on is the axis indicated by red line running down the mirrored fold, where the overlay meets and mirrors. Along this red axis, right-to-left, is where the symmetry is natural and the relationships between reflections is clearest.
Due to universal nature of this effect, even while rotating the folded axis is somehow consistently quasi-symmetrical with the opposite end of the folded axis.
Nebula NGC 2818
"Dark matterless" galaxy
Numerous gravitational lenses.
Here's is one of our own Milky Way. The similarities from left to right are very apparent.
Time lapse of the center of our galaxy where a supermassive black hole lives.
Hoag's Object
Hamilton's Object
Hamilton's Object - Wide Field 1
Hamilton's Object - Wide Field 2
If you look carefully it's even visible within the CMB.
Orion Nebula
And one more annotated one of the Milky Way. Green lines point to similar positioned star, red lines highlight repeat structure. What do you think? Sound impossible? Try to debunk it yourself and show me your results! I'm happy to be wrong. In the end I just seek a scientist who might be able to answer this! Thanks for reading!
Bonus Image! Turns out if you take a picture of the Milky Way and then place a transparent layer, instead of reversing the image, just rotate it and the Milky Way turns into a helix. Who knew!
item.dc:creator: lensinggirl

item.title: What was this guy thinking?
item.dc:creator: masteroffear

item.title: Probably not what the teacher expected for answers - a small break from the bad news out there
item.description:
1) 80-100 words
2) You can do it, answer!
3) And that is the best kind of correct.
4) This kid has a lot of pluck
5) Yes. really.
6) This kid is so stupid. I can't believe they named it Charlotte when its name is Opal.
7)
8)
9)
10) Why do birds suddenly appear, every time you are near?
11) Slava Ukraini
12) Best answer
13) we live in a digital world
14) Not true, Godzilla was created from radioactivity from nuclear bombs, they weren't around during Washington's time.
15)
16)
17) I like the way this guy thinks
18) at first I didn't see the problem, then I realized they wanted the number of objects in the blank .... I'm tired.
19) 5 points per letter, nice.
20) Obviously an American question
21) or dildos
22) Why is there a 2 by royal? Numbered the pics to easily reference them. I know some are reposts, some i had never seen before. All are enjoyable. My craft is making stupid Imgur posts and being a dad to my son.

item.title: When I came into the office and I was the only one there
item.description: I was fully prepared for it to be like last week, but luckily another colleague showed up and saved the day. But not before I could let out my inner John Travolta! And as lot of people asked for it: this dress is from Unique Vintage and unfortunately it doesn’t have pockets.

item.title: Perlers are crafty!
item.description:
My most popular piece - Hornet from Hollow Knight
Windwaker - I had to tape some beads together because I ran out of pegboards!
Zenyatta - Overwatch
Atma Weapon - Final Fantasy 6
Jurassic Park
The Reaper from Skul BEFORE the redesign - I really like the melancholy of this one
Daffy duck - Looney Tunes
Skyrim
Blade of Chaos - God of War
Cacodemon - Doom
The Simpsons
Head Crab - Half Life
Wilbur - Animal Crossing
Metroid
Legendary difficulty - Halo
Plague Doctor - Darkest Dungeon
Vivi - Final Fantasy 9
Double Sunflower - Plants Vs Zombies
Electric Mayhem - The Muppets
Futurama is brought to you by Thompsons teeth! The only teeth strong enough to eat other teeth!
Toph - Avatar the Last Airbender
Castle Crashers
Bioshock
Calvin and Hobbes
Shmebulock - Gravity Falls
GLaDOS - Portal
The Island of Myst
Ghostbusters
Daruk - Breath of the Wild
Viruses from Dr Mario 64 - I redid a lot of the coloring.
Sparkster - Rocket Knight Adventures
And here's my office! It's where I hang them all up! I recently rearranged them to make more room because I have a Shadow of the Colossus and an Over the Garden Wall piece planned and they might be a bit big. Let me know if you have any questions! Perler beads can be very Zen and honestly it's a pretty inexpensive craft/hobby

item.description: At least 20,000 port workers say they will no longer load or unload any Russian vessels or Russian cargo coming into or going out of all 29 ports up and down the West Coast. The International Longshore and Warehouse Union calls it an act of solidarity with the people of Ukraine. https://abc7.la/3sOitEz

item.title: My craft: post apocalyptic cosplay
item.description:
Apocalypse beach party! With @kikithechameleon
A lot are based on a mix of Fallout and Madmax
I wish I had the motivation to making a S-Tier cosplay like my friends here
I love shooting in ghost towns
I love that imgur has always been a friendly place for cosplay. stay weird, stay crafty.
